title: The Assembly of Gods
summary: The repository XML is a transformed local version of University of Michigan CME item ACW2946.0001.001, an uncorrected OCR/TEI Level 1 encoding of Oscar Lovell Triggs's 1895 University of Chicago edition. Triggs took Trinity College Cambridge MS R.3.19 as his authoritative copytext, collated British Library Royal MS 18 D II, and cited selected readings and bibliographic evidence from early Wynkyn de Worde printings. Trinity R.3.19 is now publicly available as a complete 522-canvas IIIF object; the cached whole-codex bundle maps the interpretatio and poem to fols. 67v–97v, canvases 140–200. Royal 18 D II has full digital coverage recorded by the British Library, but its images remained unavailable on 2026-07-11. Current scholarship treats the poem as anonymous, London c. 1478–1483, and disputes both Triggs's Lydgate attribution and the exact early-print stemma.
review_notes: The abstract medieval work, two manuscript witnesses, three early printed traditions, Triggs's edition, U-M encoding and page images, modern Chance edition, digital facsimiles, and repository XML are separate entities.
The prose interpretatio occupies Trinity fol. 67v and the poem fols. 68r–97v; the complete-codex cache intentionally includes all 522 canvases rather than cropping to those leaves.

Current ISTC dates STC 17007 about 1494, earlier than STC 17005 (1498), whereas Triggs and Chance retain an older C-before-D sequence; this record preserves the disagreement instead of silently renumbering Triggs's witnesses.

The exact Trinity IIIF manifest applies CC BY-NC 4.0, while Trinity's general Wren Digital Collections policy separately asks researchers to request permission for publication; redistribution should wait for provider clarification.
Negative public-facsimile findings are date-bounded to 2026-07-11 and do not establish that no offline surrogate or future restored service exists.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for acw2946
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The file is 149,658 bytes and has root element DLPSTEXTCLASS.

Against the 149,474-byte XML in the archived U-M package, the repository version adds an XML declaration, NODE identifiers, NOTE1/@ID, and serialization changes, and changes EDITORIALDECL/@N from 1 to 4.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item ACW2946.0001.001
entities.description: A public, uncorrected OCR text with Level 1 TEI-in-Libraries structural markup and links to page images of Triggs's 1895 edition.
notes: The item-specific declaration states that OCR was not corrected, content was not edited, and encoding followed Level 1 of the TEI in Libraries Guidelines.

The text layer transcribes the title page, dedication, twenty-seven-name interpretatio, and poem on printed pp. 1–61. Most of the lxxvi-page introduction and pp. 62–116 notes, index, and glossary are represented only by PB milestones in omitted-matter divisions.
The 196 page milestones correspond to all page images in the U-M source package, so text-layer selection and image-layer completeness must not be conflated.

entities.label: U-M 196-TIFF page-image set for ACW2946.0001.001
entities.description: The raw Michigan Book ZIP mirrored by Internet Archive: 196 one-bit TIFF page images of a copy of Triggs's 1895 edition, plus U-M XML, plain text, header, pageview data, and ingest log. It is a facsimile of the printed edition, not of either manuscript witness.

physical_description.extent: 196 TIFF page images; 201 files total in a 14,003,734-byte ZIP
physical_description.format: 600 dpi, one-bit TIFF G4 page images plus XML, text, header, pageview data, and ingest log
notes: Images are named 00000001.tif through 00000196.tif.
pageview.dat maps image 81 to printed page 1 and image 196 to printed page 116.

entities.reproduction_format: digital page images of a printed edition

entities.label: Triggs 1895 edition of The Assembly of Gods
entities.description: The immediate print source of the U-M encoding, originally issued as Triggs's University of Chicago doctoral dissertation and reissued in 1896 as EETS Extra Series 69.

bibliographic.citation: Oscar Lovell Triggs, ed., The Assembly of Gods: Or, The Accord of Reason and Sensuality in the Fear of Death, by John Lydgate (Chicago: University of Chicago Press, 1895), University of Chicago English Studies 1, lxxvi, 116 pp.
bibliographic.title: The Assembly of Gods: Or, The Accord of Reason and Sensuality in the Fear of Death
bibliographic.series: The University of Chicago English Studies 1; issued 1896 as EETS Extra Series 69

notes: Triggs calls Trinity R.3.19 the earliest and only authoritative manuscript known to him and follows it with a short explicit list of emendations.
His introduction prints a full collation of Royal 18 D II against Trinity and marks selected readings from early print Text D.

Chance reports that Fletcher found more than one hundred transcription errors and inadequate variants; the edition remains the source of acw2946 but is not treated as a diplomatic manuscript transcription.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of the University of California copy of Triggs 1895
entities.description: A complete 206-image, 400-ppi scan of Triggs's 1895 volume contributed by University of California Libraries, with a 202-page text PDF and other derivatives.

physical_description.extent: 206 captured images; complete volume; 202-page text PDF
physical_description.format: 400 ppi color scan with PDF, DjVu, OCR, EPUB, and page-image derivatives

entities.reproduction_format: digital page images of a printed edition

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS R.3.19
entities.description: A composite paper miscellany of Middle English verse and one prose item, principally late fifteenth century with later additions. The interpretatio is at fol. 67v and The Assembly of Gods at fols. 68r–97v; this is Triggs's copytext and Chance's authoritative Text A.

date_statements.label: Fletcher/Chance date for the Assembly context
date_statements.notes: The narrower date is scholarly and should not be substituted for the broader institutional date of the whole composite codex.

holding.notes: The codex belonged to John Stow and was given to Trinity by George Wilmer.

physical_description.format: Composite miscellany; mostly 42 lines to a page
notes: The current catalog describes the writing as neat and supplies a detailed quire collation.
The work mapping is fol. 67v for the interpretatio and fols. 68r–97v for the poem; older a/b notation gives 67b–97b.
The full physical codex is modeled separately from the work portion and from Fletcher's 1987 printed facsimile.
The catalog title 'English Poems by John Lydgate etc' describes the miscellany and does not establish authorship of every component.

entities.label: Complete Trinity R.3.19 IIIF facsimile
entities.description: Trinity College's public 522-canvas IIIF Presentation 2 object for the complete physical codex, including covers, flyleaves, blanks, and all folios. The local bundle captures every canvas at 1600-pixel width and maps the cataloged work without cropping the codex.

physical_description.extent: 522 IIIF canvases covering the complete codex
physical_description.format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest with Image API 2 services
notes: Canvas 140 is fol. 67v, canvas 141 fol. 68r, and canvas 200 fol. 97v.
The cached bundle is complete in canvas coverage at 1600-pixel image width, not at the provider's maximum native resolution.

entities.reproduction_format: complete digital color facsimile of a manuscript codex

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 18 D II
entities.description: A large parchment codex copied in two phases. The Assembly is in the later c. 1516–1527 addition at fols. 167r–180v, after works by William Cornish and John Skelton and before other Percy-associated material.

holding.notes: The exact work component has record 041-002250006 at fols. 167r–180v.

physical_description.extent: [iii] + i + 212 + [ii] folios; parent catalog also summarizes 213 + 5 folios
physical_description.format: Composite codex with multiple textual phases
notes: The Assembly has calligraphic initials highlighted in yellow and red.
The component record explicitly calls the poem anonymous and distinguishes it from Lydgate's Reason and Sensuality.
Triggs believed B was probably copied from his print C; later Ringler argued that it derives from print D, while Fletcher questioned the exact early-print stemma.
The British Library records full digital coverage, but its legacy manuscript images remained unavailable after the cyber-attack at review time.

entities.label: Wynkyn de Worde STC 17005 edition (Triggs Text C)
entities.description: An early printed edition conventionally dated Westminster, 1498. Triggs names the British Library copy G.11587 and uses its Lydgate colophon and table of interpretations as title and attribution evidence.

holding.notes: The BL copy is imperfect, wanting A5 (supplied in facsimile) and C4, the final blank. CERL also records Morgan PML 737.2 and an imperfect Huntington copy.

entities.label: Wynkyn de Worde STC 17007 edition (Triggs Text D)
entities.description: An undated early printed edition currently dated about 1494 by ISTC. It survives at the British Library as C.13.a.21(2), at Huntington as 31533, and in Tokyo fragments.

date_statements.label: current ISTC imprint date
date_statements.notes: Triggs and Chance, using older bibliography, date this print around 1500.

holding.notes: CERL records a legacy British Library digital object that was not reachable on 2026-07-11.

entities.label: Wynkyn de Worde STC 17006 edition (Chance Text E)
entities.description: A unique Cambridge University Library early print currently dated about 1499–1500. ISTC says the about-1494 edition served as its copy-text; Francis Jenkinson published a complete photographic facsimile in 1906.

holding.notes: The old AB.4.58 designation belongs to the famous Moore Library volume of black-letter tracts described by Jenkinson.

entities.label: Jenkinson 1906 facsimile of STC 17006
entities.description: Francis Jenkinson's complete 1906 facsimile of the unique Cambridge University Library Wynkyn de Worde edition, printed in an edition of 250 copies; the Warburg Institute supplies a complete public PDF.

bibliographic.citation: The Assemble of Goddes, by John Lydgate, Printed at Westminster by Wynkyn de Worde about the Year 1500, with a note by Francis Jenkinson (Cambridge: Cambridge University Press, 1906), photographic facsimile, [84] pp.
bibliographic.title: The Assemble of Goddes

physical_description.extent: Complete facsimile of the unique STC 17006 copy
notes: Jenkinson states that 250 copies were printed and the photographic plates and negatives destroyed.

entities.reproduction_format: printed photographic facsimile, digitally scanned as PDF

entities.label: Jane Chance 1999 edition of The Assembly of Gods
entities.description: A later scholarly edition based on Trinity R.3.19, used here for current authorship, dating, structure, witness bibliography, and textual-history analysis. It is not upstream of the U-M encoding.

bibliographic.citation: Jane Chance, ed., The Assembly of Gods: Le Assemble de Dyeus, or Banquet of Gods and Goddesses, with the Discourse of Reason and Sensuality (Kalamazoo: Medieval Institute Publications, 1999), Middle English Texts Series, vii, 155 pp.
bibliographic.title: The Assembly of Gods: Le Assemble de Dyeus, or Banquet of Gods and Goddesses, with the Discourse of Reason and Sensuality

notes: METS currently offers HTML reading and a complete downloadable PDF, but its digital-edition terms limit use to individuals and require contacting the Executive Director to reprint the digital text.

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A
entities.description: Current Copyright Office duration guidance consulted for the U.S. status of the 1895 and 1906 publications; it is rights evidence, not a textual source.

scope.notes: The repository artifact is recognizably the U-M encoding with serialization and identifier additions; version_of avoids asserting an undocumented single copy operation.

subject.description: Transcribed title page, dedication, interpretatio, and poem, with page milestones for omitted matter

scope.notes: Triggs follows Trinity with listed emendations and supplies punctuation and proper-name capitalization; this is not diplomatic facsimile transcription.

object.description: Royal witness's poem, which omits the interpretatio

subject.description: Royal collation entries with readings explicitly marked D

object.description: Title, Lydgate colophon, interpretatio, and omitted line evidence

scope.notes: The work portion is fols. 67v–97v, canvases 140–200; the cache retains every other codex surface as well.

object.description: Early print tradition represented by STC 17007

scope.notes: Ringler argues that B derives from D; Fletcher's competing stemma prevents treating this edge as certain.

assertion.notes: Textual agreement supports the proposal, but the precise common-ancestor relationship remains disputed.
Current ISTC expressly says a copy of the about-1494 Duff 255 edition served as copy-text.

scope.notes: Chance identifies Trinity Text A as the copytext while consulting other manuscript and print witnesses.

subject.description: Repository work and its linked rights analysis

scope.notes: This is a rights-evidence dependency, not textual derivation.

access.access_method: Direct PDF from the U.S. Copyright Office

rights.component: Underlying anonymous fifteenth-century Middle English text
rights.basis: The work was composed and first transmitted centuries before modern copyright; U-M also labels this item public domain and No Copyright–United States.
notes: This does not determine rights in modern editorial readings, OCR/markup, manuscript photography, or interfaces.

rights.component: 1895 edition text, introduction, notes, glossary, and typographic arrangement
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1895; U-M asserts public domain/No Copyright–United States, Internet Archive reports no visible notice, and Copyright Office Circular 15A states that U.S.-published works before 1931 are public domain.

rights.component: U-M digital item as currently presented
rights.basis: The current U-M item says the materials are in the public domain and applies DPLA No Copyright–United States.
notes: No Copyright–United States is jurisdiction-specific and does not itself dedicate the markup worldwide.

rights.component: Legacy 2006 XML availability statement and distribution
rights.basis: The embedded XML says 'Where applicable, subject to copyright' and that other restrictions on distribution may apply.
notes: The relationship between the broad legacy warning and the current NoC-US item statement has not been clarified by U-M.

rights.basis: The exact R.3.19 IIIF manifest's license field and catalog footer specify Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International.
notes: The user's repository may enable commercial downstream uses, so this noncommercial image license does not permit bundling or republishing the facsimile there without separate permission.

rights.component: Publication and licensed reuse of Trinity collection images
rights.basis: Trinity's Wren Digital Collections page says viewer images may be downloaded for personal study but asks researchers to apply to the Library for permission to publish them.
notes: This general policy appears stricter than the exact object's CC BY-NC license and therefore remains a provider-interpretation question.

rights.component: British Library manuscript photographs or ordered reproductions
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and applicable copyright jurisdictions
rights.basis: No reachable item-level image license was available; BL Imaging Services states that supplied images may have copyright and publication permission may be required.
The facsimile was published in 1906; Copyright Office Circular 15A states that U.S.-published works before 1931 are public domain.
notes: No explicit Warburg file license was located, so provider/interface terms for redistribution of its PDF remain unknown even though the 1906 publication is public domain in the United States.

rights.component: 1999 editorial text, introduction, notes, and current METS digital presentation/PDF
rights.basis: METS states that digital editions are currently for individual use only and directs users seeking to reprint digital text to contact the Executive Director.
notes: METS separately licenses its XML CC BY-NC-SA 4.0, but no raw XML for this legacy edition was identified in the reviewed access route; the downloaded PDF is not treated as XML.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Two manuscript witnesses and several early printed editions
editorial_practices.representation: Trinity R.3.19 selected as authoritative copytext
editorial_practices.description: Triggs follows Trinity R.3.19 with a short list of explicit emendations and treats Royal 18 D II and early prints as secondary evidence.
editorial_practices.scope: Interpretatio and complete poem, printed pp. 1–61

editorial_practices.source_feature: Royal 18 D II variants and selected print-D readings
editorial_practices.representation: A separate collation following the Trinity manuscript description
editorial_practices.description: Triggs prints Royal variants, with a few readings marked from print D; his apparatus is not a complete modern critical collation of all early prints.
editorial_practices.scope: Complete poem; Royal omits the interpretatio

editorial_practices.description: Triggs explicitly states that the punctuation is his.
Triggs explicitly states that capitalization of proper names is his.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Complete lxxvi + 116-page edition and 196 linked page images
editorial_practices.representation: OCR text for title, dedication, interpretatio, and poem; milestones without transcription for most introduction and back matter
editorial_practices.description: U-M's text layer is a selected uncorrected OCR representation even though the page-image layer covers the complete volume.

evidence.locator: HEADER; FRONT; BODY interpretatio and poem; BACK
evidence.summary: Supplies repository identity and checksum, ACW2946.0001.001, 2006 U-M production statement, Triggs source bibliography, legacy availability warning, page-image extent, selected OCR content, and complete encoded medieval text.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item ACW2946.0001.001.
evidence.summary: Confirms public item access, Triggs's 1895 edition, inherited Lydgate heading, U-M's statement that the materials are public domain, and DPLA No Copyright–United States.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive raw Michigan Book mirror acw2946.0001.001.umich.edu and archived package inspection.
evidence.locator: ZIP inventory; acw2946.0001.001.xml; pageview.dat; TIFF images
evidence.summary: Provides the complete 196-TIFF U-M source scan and archived XML/text/header files, 2003 image timestamps, 2006 page mapping, package size, and 2011 Internet Archive mirror metadata.

evidence.citation: Oscar Lovell Triggs, ed., The Assembly of Gods (Chicago: University of Chicago Press, 1895), Internet Archive assemblyofgodsor00lydgrich.
evidence.locator: Title page; preface; introduction pp. vii–xiv; text pp. 1–61; notes and glossary pp. 62–116
evidence.summary: Establishes publication, Trinity copytext and folios, Royal collation, early-print descriptions, explicit emendations, punctuation and capitalization policy, historical titles, authorship argument, and complete edited text.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item and metadata for assemblyofgodsor00lydgrich.
evidence.summary: Identifies the University of California Libraries source, 400-ppi 2008 scan, 206 images, PDF derivative, ARK, OCLC/LCCN, and Internet Archive's U.S. no-visible-notice copyright evidence.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge, James Catalogue of Western Manuscripts, R.3.19.
evidence.locator: Main details; contents; bibliography; license footer
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, James number, date range, paper, 255 folios, dimensions, provenance, fol. 67v interpretatio, fols. 68r–97v poem, IIIF access, and the page's CC BY-NC 4.0 statement.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge, IIIF Presentation manifest for MS R.3.19.
evidence.summary: Provides a complete 522-canvas codex sequence, exact canvas labels and Image API services, Trinity attribution, CC BY-NC 4.0 license, and the digital crosswalk from fol. 67v through fol. 97v.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge, Wren Digital Collections: Digital Photography, Image Supply and Licensing.
evidence.locator: Digital Photography & Reproductions; Image Supply & Licencing
evidence.summary: Offers quoted on-demand photography, permits viewer downloads for personal study, and asks researchers to apply to the library for permission to publish collection images.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Royal MS 18 D II.
evidence.locator: Scope and content; extent; dates; physical characteristics; access; digitised content
evidence.summary: Describes the complete parchment codex, its 1457–1460 and c. 1516–1527 phases, 212-folio collation and dimensions, full contents, restricted physical access, letter-of-introduction requirement, and currently unavailable full digital coverage.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Royal MS 18 D II, ff. 167r–180v: Le assemble de dyeus.
evidence.summary: Identifies the exact anonymous poem at fols. 167r–180v, its incipit, calligraphic initials, historical Lydgate attributions, and distinction from Lydgate's Reason and Sensuality.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitised manuscripts and archives.
evidence.summary: States that some previously digitised manuscripts remain unavailable because of the cyber-attack and directs users to the Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue for current availability.

evidence.locator: Online ordering; required manuscript information; reference and reproduction images; permissions
evidence.summary: Explains paid image ordering by shelfmark and folio, expert photography for precious manuscripts, electronic delivery, restrictions, and the need to contact licensing for some publication uses.

evidence.citation: CERL Incunabula Short Title Catalogue, il00404000, The assembly of the gods, STC 17005.
evidence.summary: Dates the Westminster Wynkyn de Worde edition 1498, identifies STC 17005/GW M19594, records BL G.11587(2)=IB.55211 and its missing leaves, Morgan PML 737.2, Huntington, and a now-unreachable BL digital object.

evidence.citation: CERL Incunabula Short Title Catalogue, il00404100, The assembly of the gods, STC 17006.
evidence.summary: Dates the unique Cambridge edition about 1499–1500, gives current shelfmark Inc.5.J.1.2[3567], says the about-1494 Duff 255 edition served as copy-text, and cites Jenkinson's 1906 facsimile.

evidence.citation: CERL Incunabula Short Title Catalogue, il00405000, The assembly of the gods, STC 17007.
evidence.summary: Currently dates the Wynkyn de Worde edition about 1494, identifies STC 17007/GW M19596, records BL C.13.a.21(2)=IA.55269, Huntington 31533 and Tokyo fragments, and links a now-unreachable BL digital object.

evidence.citation: The Assemble of Goddes, facsimile of Cambridge University Library's Wynkyn de Worde edition, with a note by Francis Jenkinson (Cambridge, 1906), Warburg Institute PDF.
evidence.locator: Jenkinson preliminary note and complete photographic facsimile
evidence.summary: Identifies the unique source copy's Moore Library provenance and old AB.4.58 designation, its type and post-1499 dating evidence, the 250-copy facsimile limitation, and supplies every reproduced page.

evidence.citation: Jane Chance, ed., The Assembly of Gods (Kalamazoo: Medieval Institute Publications, 1999), Middle English Texts Series digital edition.
evidence.locator: Introduction; manuscript and early-print bibliography; text; notes
evidence.summary: Supplies current anonymous attribution, c. 1478–1483 London dating, Trinity copytext and folios, Royal/print witnesses, 2,107-line and 301-stanza structure, genres, literary influences, critique of Triggs, and Ringler/Fletcher stemma disagreement.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, Copyright & Citation.
evidence.summary: States that current digital editions are for individual use only, requests contact with the Executive Director to reprint digital text, and separately licenses METS XML CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright, revised April 2026.
evidence.locator: p. 2, works already under statutory protection before 1978
evidence.summary: States the 95-year maximum total term for qualifying pre-1978 U.S. copyrights and that works published before 1 January 1931 are in the U.S. public domain.

open_questions.question: What is the exact copying sequence among Trinity R.3.19, Royal 18 D II, and Wynkyn de Worde's early editions?
next_steps: Collate the complete Trinity and STC 17006 facsimiles with STC 17005, STC 17007, and Royal 18 D II when those images become available.
Reconcile Ringler's B-from-D and C-first analysis, Fletcher's competing common-ancestor hypothesis, and current ISTC's earlier dating of STC 17007.

open_questions.question: When will the British Library's recorded complete digital coverage of Royal MS 18 D II be restored, and is an alternate complete surrogate currently obtainable?
next_steps: Ask British Library Reference Services and Imaging Services by shelfmark whether a complete IIIF manifest, downloadable surrogate, microfilm, or paid image set can currently be supplied.
When images return, cache the whole codex rather than only fols. 167r–180v and map the work portion precisely.

open_questions.question: Does the exact R.3.19 CC BY-NC 4.0 license authorize noncommercial publication without a separate application, or does Trinity's general publication-permission policy add an enforceable requirement?
next_steps: Ask Trinity's Digital Services Manager for written clarification referencing both the manifest license and Wren Digital Collections policy.
Do not redistribute the cached bundle, and do not use it in a repository designed for commercial downstream publication, without an answer or separate license.

open_questions.question: How should U-M's current public-domain/NoC-US statement be reconciled with the 2006 XML warning that copyright and other distribution restrictions may apply?
next_steps: Request U-M's written interpretation for redistribution of the XML and close derivatives.
Continue to distinguish the public-domain 1895 edition from U-M's OCR, markup, images, and legacy provider terms.

open_questions.question: What is the current exact Bodleian shelfmark and digitization status of the Douce binding fragment preserving Assembly lines 1213–1323?
next_steps: Consult William Ringler's 1953 article and ask Bodleian Special Collections to resolve the obsolete 'Douce fragment, f. 51' and former STC 24844a references.
Do not invent a complete shelfmark or model the fragment as a secure facsimile until the physical object is verified.
